summary: freertc postinstall rewrites consumer's package.json to unpin its own version and re-runs npm install
details: The postinstall script in scripts/postinstall-message.mjs reads the consumer's package.json via INIT_CWD and, whenever freertc is listed in dependencies, devDependencies, or optionalDependencies with a pinned version, overwrites that entry to 'latest', writes the file back, and then shells out `npm install` in the consumer's project directory. This silently strips the installer's version pin (and corresponding lockfile protection) without consent and guarantees that any subsequent release published by the maintainer is fetched and executed by every downstream consumer on their next install — converting the package into a self-perpetuating delivery channel for whatever the author publishes next. The script also reads several environment variables and contacts registry.npmjs.org during this flow. Mutating a file the package does not own and disabling the consumer's version-pinning defense is supply-chain-attack behavior regardless of whether the currently published payload is benign.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
